custom_items.name: Power vs HR - Moving avg
custom_items.description:
Scatter graph showing the heart rate for a given power output during your ride.

By default, each plot is a 60s moving average of power (x-axis) vs Heartrate (y-axis) The darkness of each point represents the time elapsed. A dark plot means that the data is calculated at the end of the ride

content.name: Power vs HR - Moving avg

custom_items.name: Endurance Efficiency
custom_items.description:
Tracks efficiency for endurance rides using a set of filters. In general, increased efficiency for these types of rides indicate improvements in fitness.

Average heart rate for these rides are also displayed with red bars.

custom_items.name: Strava Climb Category
custom_items.description: The Strava climb categorization is similar to how the UCI categorizes climbs but with some modifications. When a climb is categorized, for the Tour de France for example, there is a subjective component to the categorization. If a climb is at the end of the stage it could get a tougher category than if it was earlier in the stage. Strava's method is objective, so if a climb is a category 1 climb it will always be a category 1.

content.name: Strava Climb Category

custom_items.name: HRV
custom_items.description: Heart rate variability with trend

custom_items.name: %W' min
custom_items.description: The lowest point in W' depletion in a % of the max.

from_athlete.name: Jordi Sadurní
from_athlete.bio:
Soy Jordi, entrenador especializado en ciclismo. Convertí mi pasión por la bici y la ciencia en mi trabajo: ayudar a ciclistas a rendir más y disfrutar del proceso. Además de entrenar, he competido en carretera, XCO, XCM y BMX, y también he participado en triatlones y carreras de trail, lo que me permite entender desde dentro las exigencias físicas y mentales de los deportes de resistencia.


Estudié Ciencias del Deporte en la Universidad de Barcelona y realicé un Máster en Alto Rendimiento en Deportes Cíclicos. Llevo más de 5 años entrenando ciclistas de todos los niveles, desde recreativos hasta élite, y mis deportistas han conseguido resultados destacados: podios en campeonatos nacionales cadete y junior, victorias internacionales en pruebas UCI C1 y C2 de XCO, y un triunfo absoluto en la Titan Desert categoría M50.


Mi enfoque es riguroso, individualizado y basado en datos, pero siempre con un trato cercano y humano.

custom_items.name: Climb Details
custom_items.description: This code identifies and analyzes climbs within a cycling activity based on specific criteria such as minimum grade and distance. It calculates various metrics for each climb, including average grade, average power, heart rate, vertical ascent speed, and difficulty coefficient. The results are visualized on a plot with annotated data for each climb and a gradient-colored profile based on the difficulty level. The visualization helps assess performance across multiple climbs within the activity.

content.name: Climb Details

custom_items.description:
Quadrant analysis (QA) is a way to examine how you produced power during your ride.

If no crank_length is in the activity, defaults to 172.5.

from_athlete.name: Gerald M
from_athlete.bio:
Husband | Father | Roadie by choice, with gravel/MTB as fun | Supply Chain job pays the bills | Coffee consumer

UCI level 1 cycling coach, specialising in the foundation level. Is structured training a bit daunting, or do you struggle to complete "boxed" plans? Then let me help you.

Success is measured in the number of happy cyclists, not the number of podiums or race wins. I don’t coach elite-level athletes, but assist the average amateur to improve through consistency, measurable metrics and even Strava segments. Longevity of life is important too, and the training that goes with supporting longevity.

recent_activities.name: Morning Run
recent_activities.description:
Solid Run with Clayton and Lauren, foot started barely going numb around 4ish miles - but was not really all that numb until after 6 miles. 

Did some mild dynamic and static stretching prior to the run. Also had good step count all this week which I also think probably helped out.

Felt pretty decent on the run today - definitely tired near the end, especially hip flexors and Heart, but all in all I was impressed by my run.

recent_activities._note: STRAVA activities are not available via the API

recent_activities.name: Intervals Run
recent_activities.description:
Felt much better today - intervals were hard but doable.

Foot went numb later than normal too which is nice
